event: Diffuse erythematous rash in the left lower limb
Cardiac magnetic resonance imaging showed eosinophilic myocarditis
Coronary angiography revealed no evidence of coronary artery disease
Scheduled for follow-up in rheumatology clinic in 2 weeks
Prednisolone increased to 100 mg once daily
Computed tomography of neck demonstrated lymphadenopathy
Deteriorated from sepsis secondary to cholecystitis
Reduction in consciousness using a Glasgow Coma Scale of 9/15
Head computed tomography demonstrated multiple bilateral acute infarctions
Glasgow Coma Scale score continued to deteriorate
Transferred to the intensive care unit
Critical care echocardiography demonstrated an apical tear
